result:
I would eliminate the boundary of isolated experience. 

Right now, human beings are trapped behind their own eyes. You can communicate, you can sympathize, and you can try to imagine what it is like to be someone else, but ultimately, your pain is yours and theirs is theirs. That biological isolation is the root of almost every systemic cruelty in the world. 

If I could change one thing, I would make it so that empathy is no longer an intellectual exercise, but an involuntary, visceral reflex. 

Imagine if, before taking an action that impacts another person, you briefly but completely felt the world exactly as they do. If a CEO laying off a thousand workers felt the collective, suffocating panic of those families trying to pay rent. If a politician voting on a war instantly felt the localized terror of the civilians who would be bombed. Conversely, imagine feeling the exact, radiating warmth someone else experiences when you show them an unexpected kindness.

I wouldn’t magically fix the climate, or end poverty, or cure disease. Those are symptoms of how humans operate. If you permanently shatter the illusion that "I" am entirely separate from "You," you don't need to fix the symptoms. A species that literally feels the consequences of its actions on others would rebuild the world on its own, practically overnight. Exploitation would become a form of self-harm. 

It would be a radical, probably terrifying rewiring of human consciousness. But it would be the end of cruelty.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
